title: PCR Using Q5U Hot Start High-Fidelity DNA Polymerase (NEB #M0515): General PCR, USER®Cloning, dUTP incorporation/Carryover prevention
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"a97i7","text":"Q5U Hot Start High-Fidelity DNA Polymerase is a modified version of Q5®High-Fidelity DNA Polymerase, a novel thermostable DNA polymerase that possesses 3′ to 5′ exonuclease activity, and is fused to a processivity-enhancing Sso7d domain. Q5U contains a mutation in the uracil-binding pocket that enables the ability to read and amplify templates containing uracil and inosine bases.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":70,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"o8bb","text":"General Guidelines:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a609r","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"docea","text":"1. Template:  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"au9kh","text":"Use of high quality, purified DNA templates greatly enhances the success of PCR.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4hu5u","text":"Recommended amounts of DNA template for a 50 µl reaction are as follows:  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"79mgd","text":"DNAAMOUNTDNA Genomic1 ng – 1 µgPlasmid or Viral1 pg – 1 ng","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":58}],"data":[]},{"key":"4ks6f","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b2jhq","text":"2. Primers: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"evdet","text":"Oligonucleotide primers are generally 20 – 40 nucleotides in length and ideally have a GC content of 40 – 60 %. Computer programs such as Primer3 can be used to design or analyze primers. The best results are typically seen when using each primer at a final concentration of 0.5 µM in the reaction.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bj1bi","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bj3iq","text":"3. USER DNA Engineering","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":23}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"344r2","text":"Target DNA molecules and cloning vector are generated by PCR with 8 – 12 bases of homology between two fragments. PCR primers start with a 5′ A and contain a single deoxyuracil residue (dU) flanking the 3′ end of the homology region, and can be designed to accommodate multiple-fragment assembly, nucleotide substitutions, insertions and/or deletions. We recommend using the GeneDesign (http://genedesign.thruhere.net/gd/) software to design primers for USER junctions. The best results are typically seen when using each primer at a final concentration of 0.5 µM.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5t311","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"36ruc","text":"4. Mg++and additives:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":21},{"style":"sup","offset":5,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f4v1a","text":"Typically, the Mg++concentration for Q5U Hot Start High-Fidelity DNA Polymerase should be 2.0 mM. When used at a final concentration of 1X, the Q5U Reaction Buffer provides this optimal Mg++concentration. The addition of common PCR additives such as DMSO may improve amplification of certain difficult or long targets. In these cases, we recommend the addition of up to 2 % DMSO.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":17,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":188,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a3acu","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"441fs","text":"5. Deoxynucleotides:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":20}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6598h","text":"The final concentration of dNTPs is typically 200 μM of each deoxynucleotide.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5c70j","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3sit7","text":"6. dUTP Incorporation/Carryover Prevention","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":42},{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":42}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f1mao","text":"Q5U Hot Start High-Fidelity DNA Polymerse is a dUTP-tolerant DNA polymerase that efficiently incorporates dUTP and amplifies uracil-containing substrates. To prevent carryover contamination, dUTP and Antartic Thermolabile UDG (NEB #M0372) can be added to the reaction. dTTP can be fully replaced by dUTP in the amplification of certain targets. For best results, we recommend adding dUTP at a final concentration of 200 μM. For UDG activation, a 10 minute, 25 °C incubation step should be added before the initial denaturation step. Typical cycling parameters can be used thereafter.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fgqi6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"85of5","text":"7. Q5U Hot Start High-Fidelity DNA Polymerase concentration:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":60},{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":60}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3u5b4","text":"We generally recommend using Q5U Hot Start High-Fidelity DNA Polymerase at a final concentration of 20 units/ml (1.0 unit/50 μl reaction). However, the optimal concentration of Q5U Hot Start High-Fidelity DNA Polymerase may vary from 10 – 40 units/ml (0.5 – 2.0 units/50 μl reaction) depending on amplicon length and difficulty. It is rarely helpful to exceed 2.0 units/50 μl reaction, especially for amplicons longer than 5 kb.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f1lsi","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1kln8","text":"8. Buffers:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"64g04","text":"The 5X Q5U Reaction Buffer provided with the enzyme is recommended as the first-choice buffer for robust, high-fidelity amplification. The 5X Q5U Reaction Buffer contains 2.0 mM Mg++at a final (1X) concentration.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":180,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6s1pi","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e9mev","text":"9. Denaturation:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":16},{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"70uka","text":"Q5U Hot Start High-Fidelity DNA Polymerase does not require a separate activation step.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":87}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5cd9b","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fdl9p","text":"An initial denaturation of 30 seconds at 98 °C is sufficient for most targets being amplified from pure DNA templates. Longer initial denaturation times can be used (up to 3 minutes) for templates that require it. During thermocycling, the denaturation step should be kept to a minimum. Typically, a 5 – 10 second denaturation at 98 °C is recommended for most templates.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8kk27","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b5t2","text":"10. Annealing:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6dvkj","text":"Optimal annealing temperatures for Q5U Hot Start High-Fidelity DNA Polymerase tend to be higher than for other PCR polymerases. The NEB Tm Calculator should be used to determine the annealing temperature when using this enzyme. A temperature gradient can also be used to optimize the annealing temperature for each primer pair.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":137,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"973vc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"78eu3","text":"For high Tm primer pairs, two-step cycling without a separate annealing step can be used (see note 11).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":10,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cnicl","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9r62t","text":"11. Extension:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":14},{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c9s8s","text":"The recommended extension temperature is 72 °C. Extension times are generally 20 – 30 seconds per kb for complex, genomic samples. Extension time can be increased to 1 minute per kb for long, complex templates, if necessary.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9sfn6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1d9rs","text":"A final extension of 5 minutes at 72 °C is recommended.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1cgcm","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dc7ms","text":"12. Cycle number:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":17},{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cvpao","text":"Generally, 30–35 cycles yield sufficient product. For genomic amplicons, 30 cycles are recommended.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5au8i","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"eh6hn","text":"13. 2-step PCR:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6rlhf","text":"When primers with annealing temperatures ≥ 72 °C are used, a 2-step thermocycling protocol (combining annealing and extension into one step) is possible.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"diunl","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c1kvr","text":"14. Amplification of long products:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":35}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7es5r","text":"When amplifying products \u003e 6 kb, it is often helpful to increase the extension time to 1 minute /kb.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3op6u","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"auqjg","text":"15. PCR product:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"90h0n","text":"The PCR products generated using Q5U Hot Start High-Fidelity DNA Polymerase have blunt ends. If cloning is the next step, then blunt-end cloning is recommended. If T/A-cloning is preferred, the DNA should be purified prior to A-addition, as Q5U Hot Start High-Fidelity DNA Polymerase will degrade any overhangs generated.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a1lhf","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4miff","text":"Addition of an untemplated -dA can be done with Taq DNA Polymerase (NEB #M0267) or Klenow exo– (NEB #M0212).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":47,"length":4},{"style":"sup","offset":93,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":72,"length":6},{"key":2,"offset":100,"length":6}],"data":[]},{"key":"3ipvb","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"77i0b","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5231s","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"13rjo","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"tables","mutability":"","data":{"cellsMeta":{"0_0":"","0_1":"","1_0":"","1_1":"","2_0":"","2_1":""},"colWidths":[50,50],"data":[["DNA","AMOUNT"],["DNA Genomic","1 ng – 1 µg"],["Plasmid or Viral","1 pg – 1 ng"]],"guid":"D0ED0930E1E011E9913F21B168CC1F15","legend":"","mergeCells":[],"rowHeights":[23,23,23],"show_headers":true}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"67A21770484911EAA76D8305BFF266EA","url":"https://international.neb.com/products/m0267-taq-dna-polymerase-with-thermopol-buffer#Product%20Information"}},"2":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"7B50B240484911EAA76D8305BFF266EA","url":"https://international.neb.com/products/m0212-klenow-fragment-3-5-exo#Product%20Information"}}}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"c55jo","text":"Please note that protocols with Q5U Hot Start High-Fidelity DNA Polymerase may differ from protocols with other polymerases. Conditions recommended below should be used for optimal performance.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":31,"length":43}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ed012","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"790bc","text":"Reaction Setup:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"50i6l","text":"Q5U Hot Start High-Fidelity DNA Polymerase is inhibited at room temperature, allowing flexible reaction setup (room temperature or ice).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":136}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"150o2","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b3f4f","text":"All components should be mixed prior to use. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":45}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"f01e4","text":"Please see SDS (Safety Data Sheet) for hazards and safety warnings.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Set up the reaction using the following table: %s %s Component25 µl Reaction50 µl ReactionFinal Concentration5X Q5U Reaction Buffer5 µl10 µl1X10 mM dNTPs0.5 µl1 µl200 µM10 µM Forward Primer1.25 µl2.5 µl0.5 µM10 µM Reverse Primer1.25 µl2.5 µl0.5 µMTemplate DNAvariablevariable< 1,000 ngQ5U Hot Start High-Fidelity DNA Polymerase0.25 µl0.5 µl0.02 U/µlNuclease-Free Waterto 25 µlto 50 µl Gently mix the reaction. Collect all liquid to the bottom of the tube by a quick spin if necessary. Overlay the sample with mineral oil if using a PCR machine without a heated lid.

Transfer PCR tubes to a PCR machine and begin thermocycling. Q5U Hot Start High-Fidelity DNA Polymerase does not require a separate activation step. %s %s STEPTEMPTIMEInitial Denaturation98°C30 seconds30 Cycles98°C5-10 seconds*55–72°C20 seconds72°C20-30 seconds/kbFinal Extension72°C5 minutesHold4–10°C *Use of the NEB T m Calculator is highly recommended.
creator: New England   Biolabs
